meta.title: Arc-Flash Assessment — MCC-2 480 V Motor Control Centre
meta.subtitle: Incident-energy study + arc-flash label (NFPA 70E 130.5 / IEEE 1584-2018)
meta.author: HSE Utilities & Power
meta.deid_notice: All personnel are referred to by role label. A prior arc-flash burn incident is cited at role level as context; identifiers were pseudonymised before drafting and the re-identification key is held separately, not part of this document.

sections.text: MCC-2, 480 V motor control centre, Substation 2 — bucket-replacement and feeder-breaker maintenance. The hazardous condition is an arc-flash event at the 480 V bus. The de-energization decision (below) was recorded BEFORE any PPE selection. The incident energy, arc-flash boundary, and PPE category are COMPUTED by the IEEE 1584-2018 engine, not narrated.

De-energize-first: the maintenance task CAN be done de-energized. An electrically safe work condition (NFPA 70E Article 120) is established BEFORE any reliance on arc-rated PPE — arc-rated PPE is the documented last line, never the headline control.

rows: Identify all sources feeding MCC-2 (incomer + any back-feed)
Open the upstream feeder breaker; rack out where designed
Apply lockout/tagout to the isolating device
Test-before-touch with a proven, tested voltage detector
Apply protective grounds where stored/induced energy is credible
sections.caption: Establishing an electrically safe work condition before access (de-energize first)
IEEE 1584-2018 arc-flash inputs and computed arcing current (via arcflash.py — never narrated)

items: Limited approach boundary — per the 480 V system voltage row of NFPA 70E Table 130.4 (cite the table; do not paste the cells).
Restricted approach boundary — per the same 130.4 row; entry requires a qualified person + an energized-work permit (only if energized work is later justified).
Arc-flash boundary: 1332 mm (the distance to 1.2 cal/cm²; second-degree-burn onset).

sections.text: Hierarchy-ranked controls (de-energize first, arc-rated PPE last)

rows.control: De-energize and establish an ESWC (Article 120) — the primary control for this maintenance task
Reduce clearing time via a maintenance-mode / arc-energy-reduction trip setting
Mark the 1332 mm arc-flash boundary on the floor and update the panel label
CAT 2 arc-rated PPE as the residual-hazard last line during the isolation/verification step

sections.text: Arc-rated PPE is NOT the primary control. A treatment that leads with 'issue a CAT 2 suit' on a task that can be de-energized is a FLAG pushed up the hierarchy — the de-energization decision comes first; the CAT 2 rating is the residual protection during isolation/verification.

rows: study date to be stamped at issue
sections.caption: Equipment arc-flash warning-label content per NFPA 70E 130.5(H)

rows.action: Install the updated arc-flash label with the computed boundary and category
Commission the maintenance-mode arc-energy-reduction trip setting
Refresh the arc-flash study on any protection or system change

items: Bolted fault current (22.6 kA) and clearing time (0.2 s) taken from the supplied short-circuit/protection study — a missing input would be a [GAP], never an invented value.
India site: state electricity-rule return owed — [GAP] (resolve the state via hse-india; no national form number minted).
NFPA 70E (2024) — 130.5 (arc-flash RA), 130.5(G) (incident-energy method), 130.5(H) (label content), 130.4 (shock + approach boundaries), 130.7(C)(15) (PPE-category table), Article 120 (ESWC)
IEEE 1584-2018 — the incident-energy + arc-flash-boundary calculation (208 V – 15 kV)
OSHA 29 CFR 1910.333 (de-energize-first / energized-work justification) + 1910.269 (T&D approach distances); UK EAWR 1989 reg 14 + HSG85; India CEA / state rules via hse-india
Review trigger: on system change / on protection change / 5-yearly — whichever is first
Decision-support only — a competent person (qualified electrical engineer) must review before use.
